notes.note: Cicero's brother Quintus, the estranged father; "his sister" and "his mother" both denote Pomponia, Atticus's sister and Quintus's wife, while "his son" is the young Quintus, the nephew at the centre of the family quarrel.
The Greek skoliais apatais ("crooked stratagems"), recurring from letters 13.38--13.39, marks Cicero's chosen tactic of evasive indirection rather than open confrontation with the nephew.
The reference to "Cana" is obscure and the text uncertain; it may name a person, place, or matter then under discussion, but no secure identification can be made. Left untagged.
M. Iunius Brutus, whose added weight in the matter would, Cicero says, settle the question beyond doubt.
